nodes.title: Declare the tablist relationship
nodes.why: The tablist role gives assistive technology the correct grouping for the interactive tab controls.
nodes.alternative: Use visually styled buttons with no shared navigation semantics.
nodes.tradeoff: ARIA relationships add attributes to maintain, but they expose the component structure beyond visual styling.

nodes.title: Publish selected state on each tab
nodes.why: The accessible selected state must update from the same activeTab source that drives styling and panels.
nodes.alternative: Change only the active CSS class and leave screen readers with stale state.
nodes.tradeoff: The binding repeats per button, but it keeps visual and semantic state synchronized.

nodes.title: Render panels from active state
nodes.why: Built-in @if ensures inactive panels leave the DOM so exactly one tabpanel is exposed.
nodes.alternative: Render every panel and hide inactive content only with CSS.
nodes.tradeoff: Conditional rendering recreates panel DOM on selection, but it produces a simpler accessibility tree.

nodes.title: Use one typed active-tab source
nodes.why: A single TabId value drives active classes, aria-selected, and panel visibility without duplicated booleans.
nodes.alternative: Maintain a separate boolean for each tab and panel.
nodes.tradeoff: A union type is slightly more formal, but it prevents impossible multi-active states.
